name: Ascent to Pico Pelado from Casablanca
name_original: Ascenso al Pico Pelado desde Casablanca
region_intro: Hiking around Abarán offers diverse landscapes, from tranquil riverside paths along the Segura River to more challenging mountain trails in the Sierra del Oro and Sierra de la Pila. The region is characterized by its unique ancient water wheels, fertile orchards of the Ricote Valley, and prominent hills providing panoramic views. This area provides a mix of terrain suitable for various hiking experiences. Best hiking trails around Abarán The most popular hiking route is The Norias Trail (PR-MU 28) — Circular from Abarán, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 6 minutes to complete, exploring the historic water wheels and riverside scenery. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to the Mirador de la cruz de Abarán (PR-MU 56), a moderate 3.4 miles (5.4 km) path. This route leads to a viewpoint offering expansive vistas of the valley. Local hikers also love the Jarral Dam – Noria Grande loop from Abarán, a 7.0 miles (11.2 km) trail leading through riverside areas and past water features, often completed in about 2 hours 55 minutes. Hiking around Abarán is defined by the Segura River, the Ricote Valley, and the Sierra del Oro mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more strenuous mountain ascents. The routes in Abarán are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 900 hikers have used komoot to explore Abarán's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Abarán?
A: Abarán offers a diverse selection of around 15 hiking routes. These trails cater to various preferences, ranging from easy riverside strolls to more challenging mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for every hiker.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Abarán?
A: Yes, Abarán has several easy trails perfect for beginners and families. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893422220/the-norias-trail-pr-mu-28-circular-from-abaran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Norias Trail (PR-MU 28)&lt;/a&gt; is an easy, circular route that explores the historic water wheels and tranquil riverside scenery along the Segura River.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Abarán?
A: Absolutely. Many of Abarán's trails are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21851264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jarral Dam – Noria Grande loop from Abarán&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through scenic riverside areas and past water features.

Q: Are there any trails with viewpoints in Abarán?
A: Yes, for panoramic views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899968140/ascent-to-the-mirador-de-la-cruz-de-abaran-pr-mu-56" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to the Mirador de la Cruz de Abarán (PR-MU 56)&lt;/a&gt;. This moderate route leads to a prominent viewpoint offering expansive vistas of the Ricote Valley and the surrounding landscape. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4859719" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Cross of Abarán&lt;/a&gt; as a highlight.

Q: What natural attractions or landmarks can I see while hiking in Abarán?
A: Abarán's trails often feature unique natural and historical landmarks. You can explore the ancient water wheels along the Segura River, visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5051429" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Acequia de la Andelma&lt;/a&gt;, or discover the peculiar stony landscapes of the Rambla de Benito. The Sierra del Oro also offers stunning natural beauty.

Q: Is Abarán suitable for dog-friendly hikes?
A: While specific dog regulations can vary, many natural areas and trails in Abarán are generally welcoming to dogs, especially if kept on a leash. It's always advisable to check local signage and ensure your dog is well-behaved, particularly around wildlife and other hikers.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Abarán?
A: Thanks to its warm climate, Abarán is enjoyable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures for longer treks, while winter can provide crisp, clear days. Even in summer, early morning or late afternoon hikes along the shaded riverside paths are popular.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers in Abarán?
A: Yes, for those seeking a challenge, Abarán and its surroundings offer more strenuous options. Trails leading into the Sierra del Oro or the nearby Sierra de la Pila Regional Park provide significant elevation gains and longer distances. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e900527262/ascent-to-pico-pelado-from-casablanca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico Pelado from Casablanca&lt;/a&gt;, which is rated as difficult.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming or riverside activities after a hike?
A: Yes, the Segura River is central to Abarán's outdoor appeal. The El Jarral River Beach, a natural backwater of fresh water, is a popular spot for adventure sports and relaxation during warmer months, perfect for cooling off after a hike. Efforts have also been made here to recover native fish species.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Abarán?
A: The hiking routes in Abarán are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 170 reviews. Nearly a thousand hikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique water wheels, the tranquil riverside paths, and the stunning views from elevated points.

Q: Are there specific trails that showcase Abarán's famous water wheels?
A: Definitely. Abarán is renowned for its ancient water wheels, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e893422220/the-norias-trail-pr-mu-28-circular-from-abaran" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Norias Trail (PR-MU 28)&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight these historical structures. This circular route allows you to explore several operational water wheels, orchards, and riverside forests, showcasing the ancestral irrigation systems.

Q: Are there trails that explore the Sierra del Oro mountain range?
A: Yes, the Sierra del Oro is a prominent natural feature near Abarán, offering excellent hiking opportunities. Trails like the Camino de la Hoya del Pino and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e899968140/ascent-to-the-mirador-de-la-cruz-de-abaran-pr-mu-56" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PR-MU 56 footpath&lt;/a&gt; lead into this range, providing beautiful views and access to points like the Mirador de la Cruz de Abarán and the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora del Oro.

Q: Is parking available at the trailheads in Abarán?
A: Many popular trailheads in and around Abarán, especially those starting from the town center or well-known attractions, typically offer designated parking areas. It's always a good idea to check specific route details or local maps for the most convenient parking options for your chosen hike.
tips: Refugio Pico Pelado is situated nearby to Cerro Gordo and Sierra de la Pila. It is located on a small peak that will provide us with excellent views.
